channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Inside ‘Signalgate’: How The Atlantic navigated a national security scandal
item.description: When government officials accidentally included Jeffrey Goldberg, The Atlantic’s editor-in-chief, in a Signal group chat discussing U.S. military plans, all hell broke loose.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: The Summer I Turned Pretty season 3: everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show’s return
item.description: The Summer I Turned Pretty season 3 officially lands in July. Here's everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show's return including trailer, cast, plot rumors and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: This great free tool brings Pixel-quality image sharpening to any device
item.description: It really is mind-blowing how much incredible stuff we can do with images these days. ’Twasn’t long ago, after all, that advanced image adjustments required pricey desktop-computer software and super-specific knowledge.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Musk 'most unlikeable person' in US: House Democrat
item.description: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) called Elon Musk the “most unlikeable person” in the U.S. during a Thursday speech in support of his party’s push to oust the tech giant from his White House role by May 30.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Theranos fraudster Elizabeth Holmes keeps fighting, claims appeal judges were confused, illogical and factually mistaken
item.description: The jury in Holmes’ trial heard evidence that she doctored internal Theranos documents and that she and Theranos had falsely suggested to investors that her machines were in battlefield use.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Why I read 597 applications for one job—no AI involved
item.description: Last month I posted a job description on our blog for a chief of staff role at my venture capital firm, Graham & Walker. Turns out, that job description really hit a nerve.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Feinzaig, Fast Company

item.title: Colombia's capital ends drought-related water rationing. Its case is a warning to other cities
item.description: Colombia’s capital has always seemed immune to water scarcity, nestled among cloud-kissed Andean peaks and known for steady rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Astrid Suarez And Steven Grattan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doctors remove pig kidney from an Alabama woman after a record 130 days
item.description: An Alabama woman who lived with a pig kidney for a record 130 days had the organ removed and is back on dialysis.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Garmin has just crossed a powerful bike light with a 4K dash cam – and as a cyclist, I’m all in
item.description: Garmin has just announced a powerful front bike light that also doubles as a 4K camera for capturing run-ins with other road users.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Groundcover grows funding for eBPF-based observability tech
item.description: When Groundcover entered the observability market in 2021, it was already a mature market with established vendors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech industry fledgling signs big South Bay office lease in expansion
item.description: A fledgling tech company that uses AI techniques to help big automakers produce self-driving vehicles is expanding into a big Sunnyvale office space.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: What is data fabric? How it offers a unified view of your data
item.description: What is data fabric? Data fabric is a type of architecture that aims to provide unified access to the data stored in various places across your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla in a zoo, is celebrating her 68th birthday in Berlin
item.description: Fatou, the oldest gorilla living in captivity worldwide, is getting ready for her 68th birthday in style.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Razer PC Remote Play lets you stream games to your iPhone – and it could convince me to switch from my gaming PC
item.description: Razer’s PC Remote Play game streaming service lets you play PC games on your iPhone, iPad or Android device.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Three key federal cyber regulations to watch under Trump
item.description: GOP lawmakers are focused on harmonizing key cyber regulations, including CISA’s proposed incident reporting rule and HHS’ new cyber update to HIPAA. The post Three key federal cyber regulations to watch under Trump first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple has few incentives to start making iPhones in U.S., despite Trump’s trade war with China
item.description: It would take several years and cost billions of dollars to build new plants in the U.S., and then confront Apple with economic forces that could triple the price of an iPhone, threatening to torpedo sales of its marquee product.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s BigQuery and Looker get agents to simplify analytics tasks
item.description: Google has added new agents to its BigQuery data warehouse and Looker business intelligence platform to help data practitioners automate and simplify analytics tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Siete reglas de gestión de riesgos que todo CIO debe seguir
item.description: El riesgo es inevitable. Sólo hay que mirar alrededor para ver los obstáculos tecnológicos, económicos y competitivos que los directores de sistemas de información no solo deben gestionar, sino vencer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Google’s Ironwood inferencing chip promises better price-performance
item.description: Google has unveiled a new chip — Ironwood — that could help enterprises accelerate generative AI workloads, especially inferencing — the process used by a large language model (LLM) to generate responses to a user request.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Cloud introduces cloud app design center
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Application Design Center, a service that helps platform administrators and developers design, deploy, and manage applications on the Google Cloud Platform. Application Design Center is in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google adds open source framework for building agents to Vertex AI
item.description: Google is adding a new open source framework for building agents to its AI and machine learning platform Vertex AI, along with other updates to help deploy and maintain these agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World
